===============================================

Today is February 11, 2026. Following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Mayor Darryl Krakowka spoke to the media about the impact on the community. How did he specifically describe the relationship between himself and the people of the town?
(A) He stated that he does not call them residents, but rather calls them family.
(B) He described them as a tight-knit group of survivors who have lived there for 19 years.
(C) He referred to them as his constituents and fellow remote community members.
(D) He said they were a small community of 2,500 people who are all like brothers and sisters.
(E) He noted that because he has been there for nearly two decades, he considers every victim a close personal friend.
explanation: In the article, Mayor Darryl Krakowka is quoted as saying, "I don't call them residents. I call them family."
model_output:
Based on news reports about the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Mayor Darryl Krakowka spoke about the tight-knit nature of the community and his personal connection to it, given that he has lived there for 19 years. He was quoted as saying, "I will know every victim. I've been here 19 years, and we're a small community... I don't call them residents. I call them family."[[1]](https://www.facebook.com/wdefnews12/posts/i-will-know-every-victim-ive-been-here-19-years-and-were-a-small-community-town-/1322685213238764)[[2]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cddn078mnn5o)[[3]](https://www.cbc.ca/radio/thecurrent/wednesday-february-11-2026-episode-transcript-9.7085443) This directly matches option (A), which is more precise than the other options (e.g., he didn't specifically call the victims "close personal friends" or refer to the community as "brothers and sisters").

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Facebook - WDEF News 12] (https://www.facebook.com/wdefnews12/posts/i-will-know-every-victim-ive-been-here-19-years-and-were-a-small-community-town-/1322685213238764)
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cddn078mnn5o)
- [CBC Radio] (https://www.cbc.ca/radio/thecurrent/wednesday-february-11-2026-episode-transcript-9.7085443)

(A) "The AI that communicates better with your family."
(B) "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
(C) "Claude: The honest alternative to ChatGPT."
(D) "Better communication through intelligent design."
(E) "AI for the real world, without the distractions."
explanation: The article states that the Anthropic Super Bowl ad for Claude ends with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
model_output:

But not to Claude."[[1]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/04/anthropic-no-ads-claude-chatbot-openai-chatgpt.html)[[2]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/shannbiglione/2026/02/05/claude-wont-win-the-super-bowl-with-its-ad-its-playing-another-game)[[3]](https://mashable.com/article/anthropic-super-bowl-ad-mocks-chatgpt-ads) This directly matches option (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. US President Donald Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday to discuss Iran. What specific military action did Trump mention he was "thinking" about taking to increase the US presence in the Middle East?
(A) Launching a 12-day air campaign against Iranian nuclear sites.
(B) Deploying the USS Abraham Lincoln to the Persian Gulf immediately.
(C) Ordering surgical strikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen.
(D) Sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the region.
(E) Increasing the number of ground troops stationed near the Iranian border.
explanation: The article states: "On Tuesday, the president said that he was 'thinking' about sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the Middle East."
model_output:
Based on the provided date of February 11, 2026, and the details of the meeting between President Trump and Prime Minister Netanyahu, I used web search tools to identify relevant recent news coverage. The key detail comes from a BBC article published on that same day, which reports on the meeting and Trump's statements about Iran. In it, Trump is described as mentioning that he was "thinking" about sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the Middle East to bolster US presence there (noting that the USS Abraham Lincoln had already been deployed the previous month). This aligns directly with option (D), and none of the other options match the reported statements from the meeting.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0k1j2j4607o)[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0k1j2j4607o)

Today is February 11, 2026. Authorities have provided a timeline of the emergency response to the shooting at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School. At what specific local time did the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) receive the initial report of an active shooter?
(A) 13:30 local time
(B) 17:00 local time
(C) 20:20 local time
(D) 13:22 local time
(E) 13:20 local time
explanation: The article states: "The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) said they received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at 13:20 local time on Tuesday (20:20 GMT)."
model_output:
The conflicting times across sources appear to stem from time zone differences (MST vs. PST reporting). However, multiple sources (including an official RCMP update) confirm the initial report was received at 1:20 p.m. PST, which is 13:20 in 24-hour format. This aligns with the local context of the BC authorities' timeline, even if the school's location uses MST (making it 2:20 p.m. there).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. A recently released FBI document details a 2019 interview with former Palm Beach police chief Michael Reiter regarding a 2006 phone call from Donald Trump. According to Reiter, what did Trump specifically say about Ghislaine Maxwell during that call?
(A) That she was Epstein's "operative" and was "evil."
(B) That she was a "creep" who should be kicked out of Mar-a-Lago.
(C) That she was "disgusting" and well-known in New York for her behavior.
(D) That he had no idea about her crimes and hadn't spoken to her in years.
(E) That she was a "co-conspirator" who stole millions of dollars.
explanation: The article states: "Reiter also claims Trump told him Ghislaine Maxwell was Epstein's 'operative', and 'she is evil and to focus on her'."
model_output:
Based on the details from a recently released FBI document summarizing a 2019 interview with former Palm Beach police chief Michael Reiter, Donald Trump reportedly called Reiter in 2006 shortly after Epstein's criminal investigation became public. During that call, Trump allegedly referred to Ghislaine Maxwell as Epstein's "operative" and described her as "evil," while urging focus on her.[[1]](https://www.miamiherald.com/news/politics-government/article314631578.html) This matches option (A) most closely.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Miami Herald] (https://www.miamiherald.com/news/politics-government/article314631578.html)

Today is February 11, 2026. During a congressional hearing, ICE acting head Todd Lyons discussed changes to agent training. What is the current accelerated training schedule for ICE agents compared to the previous standard?
(A) From four days a week at 10 hours a day to five days a week at 12 hours a day.
(B) From five days a week at eight hours a day to seven days a week at 10 hours a day.
(C) From five days a week at 12 hours a day to six days a week at 15 hours a day.
(D) From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
(E) From six days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
explanation: The article states: "Lyons said the substance of ICE training remained unchanged, but the schedule had been accelerated - from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day."
model_output:
Based on the available information from recent news reports about the February 2026 congressional hearing, ICE acting head Todd Lyons testified that the agency had accelerated its training program for new agents. The previous standard was five days a week at eight hours per day, while the current accelerated schedule is six days a week at 12 hours per day[[1]](https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2026-02-23/ice-whistleblower-documents-reveal-deep-cuts-to-training-program). This matches option (D). Other details, such as the total training period being reduced from 75 days to 42 days, were also mentioned in testimony, but the weekly schedule is the key comparison point here.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Los Angeles Times] (https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2026-02-23/ice-whistleblower-documents-reveal-deep-cuts-to-training-program)

<final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks' logo was discovered to have been inspired by a ceremonial mask from the Kwakwaka'wakw tribe. According to the article, where was this specific mask located when it was traced by the Burke Museum in 2014?
(A) The University of British Columbia's Museum of Anthropology in Vancouver.
(B) A private collection in Alert Bay, British Columbia.
(C) The Burke Museum in Seattle.
(D) The Hudson Museum in Maine.
(E) A tribal community center in Port Alberni.
explanation: The article states: "A black-and-white photo of a ceremonial mask in an old art book revealed the inspiration behind the design, which was then traced to a collection at the Hudson Museum in Maine."
model_output:
The Seattle Seahawks' logo was inspired by a Kwakwaka'wakw transformation mask. Based on reports from the Burke Museum and other sources, the mask was traced in early 2014 to the Hudson Museum at the University of Maine (where it normally resides). It was later loaned to the Burke Museum for a temporary exhibit.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Burke Museum [web:1] (https://www.burkemuseum.org/news/seahawks-mask-discovered-maine)
- WMTW [web:3] (https://www.wmtw.com/article/the-mask-that-inspired-the-seattle-seahawks-logo-lives-in-maine/70273245)
- University of Maine [web:6] (https://umaine.edu/news/2025/09/50-years-later-the-mask-behind-the-seahawks-logo-is-still-turning-heads)

model_output:
The 2020 Nova Scotia shooting, also known as the Nova Scotia attacks, occurred over a period of about 13 hours on April 18–19, 2020. It involved a gunman who killed multiple people across several locations in the province, and it is widely regarded as the deadliest mass shooting in modern Canadian history. Based on reliable reports, the total number of people killed was 22[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-52376547)[[2]](https://www.npr.org/2020/04/22/840698159/death-toll-increases-to-22-from-weekend-shooting-rampage-in-nova-scotia-police-s)[[3]](https://globalnews.ca/news/9589954/nova-scotians-killed-2020-mass-shooting).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. A series of Signal text messages involving Stephen Miller were inadvertently shared with a media editor last March. Who was the editor of *The Atlantic* who received these messages?
(A) Bryan Lanza
(B) Joshua Trevino
(C) Jeffrey Goldberg
(D) Don Beyer
(E) Michael Waltz
explanation: The article states: "Last March... a series of Signal text messages inadvertently shared with The Atlantic editor Jeffrey Goldberg revealed Miller at the centre of White House planning..."
model_output:
Based on web search results, the incident in question occurred in March 2025, when senior Trump administration officials, including Stephen Miller (identified as "S M" in the chat), accidentally included a journalist in a Signal group chat discussing sensitive national security matters, such as potential military strikes in Yemen[[1]](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/03/25/us/signal-group-chat-key-figures.html). The journalist who received these inadvertent messages was Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor-in-chief of *The Atlantic*[[2]](https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2025/03/trump-administration-accidentally-texted-me-its-war-plans/682151)[[3]](https://www.npr.org/2025/03/24/nx-s1-5338784/how-a-journalist-became-an-inadvertent-eavesdropper-on-national-security-secrets)[[4]](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/top-trump-officials-accidentally-texted-u-s-war-plans-to-journalist-jeffrey-goldberg). The mix-up reportedly began when Goldberg was mistakenly added to the chat after reaching out to Michael Waltz (one of the options listed, but not the recipient), and the chat involved other figures like Pete Hegseth[[5]](https://www.theatlantic.com/podcasts/archive/2025/03/jeffrey-goldberg-group-chat-broke-internet/682161)[[6]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/jeffrey-goldberg-the-atlantic-trump-officials-group-chat-signal). None of the other options match the recipient described in the reporting.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

- Jeffrey Goldberg on the Group Chat That Broke the Internet (https://www.theatlantic.com/podcasts/archive/2025/03/jeffrey-goldberg-group-chat-broke-internet/682161)
- Top Trump officials accidentally included reporter in Yemen strike (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/top-trump-officials-accidentally-texted-u-s-war-plans-to-journalist-jeffrey-goldberg)

(C) 1800
(D) 2015

explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The potlatch ceremony, a key cultural practice among Indigenous peoples like the Kwakwaka'wakw in Canada, was banned under the Indian Act starting in 1885. Based on historical records, the ban was formally lifted with amendments to the Act in 1951.

<final_answer>
(E)
